1. Equipment Data & Specifications

Center your visualization on the vessel.

Equipment Tag: PP01361 B2 (also referred to as 361 V002).

Type: Scraper Receiver (Major/Minor Barrel design).

Dimensions:

Major Barrel: 610 mm Internal Diameter (ID).

Minor Barrel: 508 mm ID (Note: Transcript said 58mm, but 508mm is physically required for the 20" connections described).

Design Conditions:

Pressure: 45 barg.

Temperature: -29°C to 100°C.

Operating Conditions:

Pressure: 10 to 11 barg.

Temperature: 20°C to 80°C.

Material: Carbon Steel + 3 mm Corrosion Alloy (CA) internal cladding.

Insulation: None (N O).

Trim Number: PP01 361 V2 TX1 B 03 E7.

2. The Relief and Venting System (Top Nozzles)

Look to the top of the vessel for safety and pressure monitoring.

Nozzle N6 (Pressure Safety Valve - PSV):

Start: A 3-inch Flange connects to the vessel.

Line: 3" PP01 361 GF 018 B 03 E7-P.

Reduction: The line reduces from 3-inch down to 1-inch.

Component: PSV-02 (Pressure Safety Valve).

Spec Change: Piping class changes from 150 rating to 300 rating here.

Discharge: After the PSV, a reducer expands the line from 2-inch to 4-inch.

Drain/Isolation:

The discharge line features a Ball Valve (HV-26).

Between the reducer and HV-26, there is a 1/2-inch Drain Valve (HV-27).

Exit Line: 4" PP01 500 VK 03 B 01 V8.

Destination: Flare System (NPG PP01 PRP ID 21001).

Nozzle N4 (Instrumentation):

Component: Connected to a 2-inch Solenoid Valve (HV-34).

Drain Valve (HV-27).

Exit Line: 4" PP01 500 VK 03 B 01 V8.

Destination: Flare System (NPG PP01 PRP ID 21001).

Nozzle N4 (Instrumentation):

Component: Connected to a 2-inch Solenoid Valve (HV-34).

Instrument: Pressure Gauge (PG-004).

Nozzle N7 (Instrumentation):

Component: Connected to a 2-inch Valve (HV-28).

Instrument: Pressure Gauge (PG-003).

Body Instrumentation:

Located physically between nozzles N7 and N9 is the Pig Signaler, tagged ZD-04 (Field) and ZI-004 (DCS).

3. The Small Bore Loop (Nozzles N1 & N9)

This is a loop system likely used for kicking or equalizing, involving two specific nozzles.

Nozzle N1:

Size: 2-inch.

First Valve: HV-38 (2" Ball Valve).

Line: 2" PP01 361 GF 015 B 03 E7-P.

Injection Point: Crucial Detail: Between the first valve (HV-38) and the second valve (HV-43), the line from Nozzle N9 enters here.

Second Valve: HV-43 (2" Globe Valve).

Instrument: Flow Element F402 (Field Instrument).

Third Valve: HV-96 (2" Ball Valve).

Spec Change: The line becomes 2" PP01 500 VK 02 B 01 B8.

Destination: It connects to the Flare System line (3" PP01 500 VK 01 B 01 B8).

Nozzle N9:

Size: 2-inch.

Valve: HV-42 (2" Valve).

Line: 2" PP01 361 GF 0018 B 03 E7-P.

Destination: This line loops back and connects into the N1 Line (specifically between HV-38 and HV-43).

4. The Drain System (Bottom Nozzles N5, N12, N3)

Look at the bottom of the vessel.

The Main Drain Header (Merging N12 and N5):

Nozzle N12:

into the N1 Line (specifically between HV-38 and HV-43).

4. The Drain System (Bottom Nozzles N5, N12, N3)

Look at the bottom of the vessel.

The Main Drain Header (Merging N12 and N5):

Nozzle N12:

Starts with a 4-inch Gate Valve (HV-14).

Line: 4" PP01 512 PK 61 B 03 E8-P. (Spec starts as B03E7, changes to B03E8 after valve).

Nozzle N5:

Starts with a 4-inch Gate Valve (HV-92).

Line: 4" PP01 512 PK 0061 B 03 E8-PP.

The Merge: N5 connects into the N12 line after HV-14.

Common Drain Line:

Line: 4" PP01 512 PK 01 B 03 E8-P.

Drain Point: 1-inch Drain Valve (HV-15).

Isolation: 4-inch Normally Closed (NC) Ball Valve (HV-16).

Control: 4-inch Gate Valve (LCV-97).

Spec Change: Line becomes 4" PP01 512 PK 02 B 01 M8.

Destination: Condensate Storage Vessel (NPG PP01 PRP ID 0024 001).

Nozzle N3 (Sump Drain):

Size: 2-inch.

Valve: HV-35 (2" Ball Valve).

Spec Change: Changes from B 03 E7 to B 01 H9.

Drain: 1/2-inch Normally Closed Drain Valve (HV-36).

Line: 2" PP01 567 GN 79 B 01 H9.

Check Valve: HV-37 (2" Check Valve).

Reducer: Reduces from 2-inch to 3/4-inch.

Destination: Sump (SPN HN 028).

5. The Main Outlet & Barred Tee Complex (Nozzles N10 & N2)

This involves the massive 20-inch and 16-inch piping leaving the vessel.

Nozzle N10 (Main Scraper Outlet):

Size: 20-inch.

Line: 20" PP01 361 GF 0011 AP 15L-P.

Valves:

HV-25 (20" Ball Valve).

HV-23 (20" Ball Valve).

Bleed: Between these two is a 2-inch Drain Valve (HV-24).

N10 (Main Scraper Outlet):

Size: 20-inch.

Line: 20" PP01 361 GF 0011 AP 15L-P.

Valves:

HV-25 (20" Ball Valve).

HV-23 (20" Ball Valve).

Bleed: Between these two is a 2-inch Drain Valve (HV-24).

Destination: This line flows into the Barred Tee.

The Barred Tee (BT) Connection:

The Barred Tee merges the flow from the Scraper Receiver (N10) with the flow coming from the Scraper Launcher.

Incoming from Launcher: Line 20" PP01 361 GF 07 AP 15L-PP (Details in Section 6).

Branch Out: A 16-inch line exits the Barred Tee.

Line: 16" PP01 361 GF 014 B 03 F9-P.

Valve: MOV-002 (16-inch Control Ball Valve).

Instrumentation on the 16" Line:

Temperature: TE-02 / TI-02 (2" connection).

Pressure: PI-04 / PIT-04 (1" connection) fitted with Solenoid Valve HV-46.

Corrosion Monitoring: 2" connections for CC02, CP002, CIT2, CE02.

Destination: This 16-inch line goes to the Fuel Gas Knockout Drum (NPG PP01 PRP 0801).

Nozzle N2 (Bypass/Parallel Line):

This nozzle connects the vessel to the Fuel Gas system (joining the 16" line described above).

It splits into two parallel paths:

8-inch Path: Contains two 8-inch Ball Valves (HV-29 and HV-31). Line: 8" PP01 361 GF 017 B 03 F9-P.

2-inch Path: Contains a 2-inch Ball Valve (HV-32) and a 2-inch Globe Valve (HV-33).

Termination: These paths merge and connect to the 16-inch line headed to the Fuel Gas Knockout Drum.

6. The Incoming Line (From Scraper Launcher)

This is the line feeding into the Barred Tee (upstream of the receiver).

ths merge and connect to the 16-inch line headed to the Fuel Gas Knockout Drum.

6. The Incoming Line (From Scraper Launcher)

This is the line feeding into the Barred Tee (upstream of the receiver).

Line: 20" PP01 361 GF 07 AP 15L-PP.

Main Valve: EZV-02 (20-inch Control Ball Valve).

Pig Signal: Located between EZV-02 and the Barred Tee is ZI-3 / ZS-03.

Connections on this Incoming Line (Upstream of EZV-02):

Flare Connection (3-inch):

Line: 3" PP01 361 GF 0061 B 03 E9-P.

Valves: HV-44 (3" Ball), HV-74 (3" Ball).

Drain: HV-45 (1/2" Drain Valve) located between the ball valves.

Instrument: FO5 (Flow Orifice).

Spec Change: Before the globe valve, spec is AP15L; it changes to B 03 E9.

Final Line: 3" PP01 500 VK 01 B 01 B8.

Destination: Flare System (NPG PP01 PRP 2101).

Instrumentation Taps (2-inch):

Tap 1: HV-21 (2" Solenoid Valve) connecting to PZT-05, PI-05, PI-05 (SCADA).

Tap 2: HV-22 (2" Solenoid Valve) connecting to PIT-03, PI-03.

System Overview and Visualization

Imagine standing in front of a large industrial piping assembly. The centerpiece is the Scraper Receiver, a heavy pressure vessel designed to receive pipeline cleaning tools (pigs). The system consists of a massive 20-inch main pipeline, the receiver vessel itself, and a complex network of smaller bypass, drain, and vent lines managing pressure and fluid flow.

1. Equipment Specifications: The Scraper Receiver

Tag: PP01361 P1 (also referred to as 361 V01).

Dimensions: The vessel has a Major Barrel with a 610 mm Internal Diameter (ID) for handling the pig, and a Minor Barrel with a 508 mm ID connected to the pipeline.

Material: Constructed from Carbon Steel with a 3 mm internal corrosion alloy lining.

Conditions:

Design: Rated for 45 barg pressure with a temperature range of -29°C to +100°C.

Operating: Runs between 10 to 11 barg at 20°C to 80°C.

Insulation: The vessel is uninsulated.

Trim Number: PB01 361 B0 TX B 03 E7.

2. The Main Incoming Pipeline (20-Inch Line)

Everything begins with the main product flow arriving from the New PCB Station.

Line: This is a 20-inch line (Tag: PP01 361 GF01 P 03 F9).

Instrumentation: Immediately upon entering the diagram, the flow passes flow instrumentation:

FI-01 / FY-01 (Flow Indicator and Relay).

Corrosion Injection: Downstream of the flow meter, there is a 2-inch injection point connecting to the Corrosion Inhibitor Injection Package (PB01 520 Z101).

FI-01 / FY-01 (Flow Indicator and Relay).

Corrosion Injection: Downstream of the flow meter, there is a 2-inch injection point connecting to the Corrosion Inhibitor Injection Package (PB01 520 Z101). The line includes a specific valve arrangement (CP01, CC01, CI01) and connects via pump NPG PP01.

Branching: This main 20-inch line continues past the receiver. However, to divert the pig into the receiver, flow is manipulated via the Kicker Line (Nozzle N1).

3. The Kicker Line (Nozzle N1)

This system diverts fluid to push the pig into the receiver. It taps off the main 20-inch line and enters the vessel at Nozzle N1.

Line Size: 8-inch (Tag: PP01 361 GF 03 B 03 E7).

Valve Arrangement (Double Block and Bleed):

The line passes through an 8-inch Ball Valve (HV-02).

It continues through a second 8-inch Ball Valve (HV-01).

Safety Bleed: Sandwiched between HV-02 and HV-01 is a 1-inch Drain Ball Valve (HV-03) used to depressurize the space between the valves.

Bypass Loop: Parallel to this main kicker route, there is a smaller bypass for fine control:

It features a 2-inch Globe Valve (HV-05) and a 2-inch Ball Valve (HV-04).

Piping Spec Change: Before the Globe valve, the spec is B 03 E7; after the Globe valve, it changes to B 03 F9.

Alternate Path (N11): There is also a 4-inch branch (Tag: GF05 B 03 E7) originating before HV-02. It passes through a 4-inch Valve (HV-10) and connects to Nozzle N11 (the receiving side of the barrel).

4.

nate Path (N11): There is also a 4-inch branch (Tag: GF05 B 03 E7) originating before HV-02. It passes through a 4-inch Valve (HV-10) and connects to Nozzle N11 (the receiving side of the barrel).

4. Venting, Equalization, and Relief Systems (N2, N7, N4)

To safely operate the receiver door, pressure must be managed via top-mounted nozzles.

Nozzle N2 & N7 (Balance Line):

Nozzle N2 serves as the moving/pressurization side. It is a 2-inch line equipped with a 2-inch Ball Valve (HV-36).

Nozzle N7 connects directly to the N2 line. The N7 line (Tag: GF0010) includes a 2-inch Ball Valve (HV-11).

These lines merge. After the merge, the line flows through HV-06 (Ball Valve) and HV-07 (Globe Valve).

Control Valve: Branching off HV-07 is a control valve F401. Here, the piping spec changes from B 03 E7 to B 01 B8 (Tag: PP01 502...). This line vents to a Safe Location (SPN01).

Nozzle N4 (Safety Relief):

This is a 3-inch line (GF0008) fitted with a blind flange initially.

It reduces from 3-inch to 1-inch to feed the Pressure Safety Valve (PSV-01).

PSV Details: Set pressure 45 barg, Size 1E2, Fireproof case.

Discharge: After the PSV, the line expands via a reducer (2" to 4") and vents to a safe location. This discharge line includes a 0.5-inch Drain Valve (HV-1).

5. Vessel Instrumentation (N3, N5 & Body)

Nozzle N3: Fitted with a 2-inch Solenoid Valve (HV-08) and a Pressure Gauge connection (PG-02).

This discharge line includes a 0.5-inch Drain Valve (HV-1).

5. Vessel Instrumentation (N3, N5 & Body)

Nozzle N3: Fitted with a 2-inch Solenoid Valve (HV-08) and a Pressure Gauge connection (PG-02).

Nozzle N5: Fitted with a 2-inch Solenoid Valve (HV-09) and a Pressure Gauge connection (PG-01).

Pig Signaler: Mounted directly on the vessel is Z-02 / HZI-2, which signals when a pig has arrived in the barrel.

6. The Drain System (Nozzles N10 & N12)

Located at the bottom of the vessel to drain liquids into the closed drain system.

Configuration:

Nozzle N10: 4-inch outlet with a Gate Valve. Spec changes from B 03 E7 to B 03 E8.

Nozzle N12: 4-inch outlet with a Gate Valve. Spec changes similarly.

These two lines (DK01 and DK02) merge into a common header.

Drain Header:

The combined line passes through a 4-inch Gate Valve (HV-13).

Spec Change: After HV-13, the pipe spec changes to B 01 M8 (Tag: DK03).

There is a 0.5-inch drain point (HV-09) located near the main drain valves.

Termination: The line expands via a reducer (4" to 8") and routes to the Closed Drain System (2D DC1).

7. Main Outlet and Re-injection (Nozzle N9)

This is where the pig is retrieved, and the flow reconnects to the main line.

Nozzle N9: The main barrel outlet.

Isolation:

First, a 20-inch Ball Valve (HV-16).

Second, a 20-inch Ball Valve (HV-18).

Bleed: Between these two massive valves lies a 2-inch Drain Valve (HV-17).

Nozzle N9: The main barrel outlet.

Isolation:

First, a 20-inch Ball Valve (HV-16).

Second, a 20-inch Ball Valve (HV-18).

Bleed: Between these two massive valves lies a 2-inch Drain Valve (HV-17).

Barred Tee (Bar T): The line from N9 connects to the main pipeline via a Barred Tee. This special tee fitting prevents the pig from accidentally entering the bypass line.

Main Line Control:

Downstream of the Barred Tee on the main line is the Motor Operated Valve/Control Valve EZV-01.

Pig Signaler: Between the Barred Tee and EZV-01, there is another Pig Signaler (Z-01 / ZI-01) to confirm the pig has passed or arrived.

Final Output:

After passing EZV-01, the 20-inch line piping spec changes from AP15L to GF07 AP15L.

The line continues toward the next scraper section.

Solenoid Taps: Two final 2-inch connections are located here:

HV-19: Connects to PI-02 (DCS) and PIT-02 (Field) pressure transmitters.

HV-20: Connects to PZT-04 and PI-11.

Note: The small bore piping for these instruments changes spec to B 03 F9.

1.0 Main Equipment: Fuel Gas Knockout Drum (PP01-362-V-01)

This document provides a detailed technical breakdown of the Fuel Gas Knockout Drum, designated PP01-362-V-01, and its associated piping and instrumentation systems. The knockout drum serves as a critical process vessel, designed to remove entrained liquids from two primary fuel gas streams before the gas proceeds to further treatment. This initial section details the fundamental design parameters and material specifications of the vessel itself.

The core specifications for the PP01-362-V-01 vessel are summarized below.

This foundational data provides the context for the following analysis of the primary inlet piping systems that feed this drum.

4.0 Interconnected Level Instrumentation and Drain Systems

This section analyzes the complex, interconnected piping networks that service multiple nozzle pairs associated with level measurement and control. These systems feature parallel solenoid valves and local instrumentation that ultimately merge into common drain and instrumentation headers.

4.1 Level and Drain Header Network

This network collects signals and provides drainage for three sets of level instruments, combining them into a single header line. The flow paths originate from the following nozzle pairs:

• N18A and N18B: These 2" nozzles are each equipped with a solenoid valve (HV024 and HV025, respectively). They connect to a common line featuring a level gauge (LG triple01) isolated by 1" valves (HV26 and HV27).

B: These 2" nozzles are each equipped with a solenoid valve (HV024 and HV025, respectively). They connect to a common line featuring a level gauge (LG triple01) isolated by 1" valves (HV26 and HV27).

• N7A and N7B: These 2" nozzles tie into a common line for level transmitter LIT trip01. Each nozzle is isolated by a 2" solenoid valve (HV22 and HV23). The source indicates a 1" drain valve (HV28) is present on the instrument tubing upstream of the HV22 solenoid valve. The common line is isolated by gate valve HB0029.

• N10A and N10B: These 2" nozzles are equipped with solenoid valves (HV30 and HV31). Their line includes level transmitter LIT 03 and is isolated by valves HV0032 and HV33.

The individual lines from these three systems ultimately merge and combine into a single common header line: 1 in PP01512 PK54 B 01 M8 PP.

5.1 Summary of Control Loops

The P&ID details for the PP01-362-V-01 drum indicate one primary, explicitly defined control loop and a comprehensive system for level monitoring and control.

• Pressure Control: The primary automated control loop manages the drum's operating pressure. As detailed in Section 3.10, pressure transmitter PIT02 (on Nozzle N16) sends a signal to PIC02, which in turn modulates control valve PV02 on the 8-inch process gas outlet line to the HP Flare Header.

1.1 Fuel Gas Knockout Drum (PP01-362-V001)

The central process vessel is a horizontal knockout drum designed to remove entrained liquids from fuel gas streams originating from the DS1 and DS3 scrapper receivers. The vessel separates liquid droplets from the gas phase through gravity settling and velocity reduction before the gas proceeds to the Fuel Gas Treatment Package.

SECTION 7: CONTROL LOOP DESCRIPTIONS

7.1 Pressure Control Loop (PIC-0002 → PV-0002)

Purpose: Maintains vessel operating pressure within 9.0-10.4 barg range by modulating gas flow to HP Flare.

Loop Components:

PIT-0002: Pressure transmitter at Nozzle N16 senses vessel pressure

PIC-0002: Controller compares measured pressure to setpoint

PV-0002: 8-inch control valve on line 8"-PP01-362-GF0003-B01E9-PP modulates gas flow

Control Action:

Increasing vessel pressure → PIC-0002 output increases → PV-0002 opens → More gas flows to flare → Pressure decreases

ol valve on line 8"-PP01-362-GF0003-B01E9-PP modulates gas flow

Control Action:

Increasing vessel pressure → PIC-0002 output increases → PV-0002 opens → More gas flows to flare → Pressure decreases

Decreasing vessel pressure → PIC-0002 output decreases → PV-0002 closes → Less gas flows to flare → Pressure increases

7.2 Level Control Loop (LIC-0001 → LV-0001)

Purpose: Maintains vessel liquid level at Normal Liquid Level (800mm) by modulating liquid discharge.

Loop Components:

LIT-0001: Level transmitter at Nozzles N7A/N7B senses liquid level

LIC-0001: Controller compares measured level to NLL setpoint (800mm)

LV-0001: 1.5-inch control valve on N3 outlet bypass modulates liquid flow

Control Action:

Rising level → LIC-0001 output increases → LV-0001 opens → More liquid drains → Level decreases

Falling level → LIC-0001 output decreases → LV-0001 closes → Less liquid drains → Level increases

7.3 High Pressure Safety Instrumented Function (PZT → 2oo3 → ESD)

Purpose: Provides independent high-pressure protection to prevent vessel overpressure.

Loop Components:

PZT-0001A, PZT-0001B, PZT-0001C: Three independent pressure transmitters

2oo3 Voting Logic (362-004): Requires 2 of 3 transmitters to confirm high pressure

ESD System: Initiates protective actions

PZT-0001A, PZT-0001B, PZT-0001C: Three independent pressure transmitters

2oo3 Voting Logic (362-004): Requires 2 of 3 transmitters to confirm high pressure

ESD System: Initiates protective actions

Safety Action: When 2 out of 3 transmitters detect pressure above the high-pressure trip setpoint, the voting logic sends a trip signal to the ESD system, which closes EZV-0001 and initiates other protective actions.

7.4 Level Safety Instrumented Function (LZT → 2oo3 → ESD)

Purpose: Provides independent level protection for both high-high and low-low level conditions.

Loop Components:

LZT-0002A, LZT-0002B, LZT-0002C: Three independent GWR level transmitters

2oo3 Voting Logic (362-003 for HHLL, 362-007 for LLLL): Requires 2 of 3 transmitters to confirm level alarm

ESD System: Initiates protective actions

Safety Actions:

HHLL (1300mm): Voting logic 362-003 trips to prevent liquid carryover to downstream equipment

LLLL (300mm): Voting logic 362-007 trips to prevent pump cavitation or loss of seal

1.1 Fuel Gas Knockout Drum (PP01-362-V001)

The central process vessel is a horizontal knockout drum designed to remove entrained liquids from fuel gas streams originating from the DS-1 and DS-3 scrapper receivers. The vessel separates liquid droplets from the gas phase through gravity settling and velocity reduction before the gas proceeds to the Fuel Gas Treatment Package.

1.1.5 Vessel Internals

The vessel contains a mist eliminator designated as MW 24" (24-inch Mist Wire mesh demister pad). This wire mesh pad is positioned in the gas flow path upstream of the gas outlet nozzle N2. The demister captures entrained liquid droplets from the gas stream before the gas exits the vessel, improving separation efficiency.

SECTION 7: CONTROL LOOP DESCRIPTIONS

7.1 Pressure Control Loop (PIC-0002 → PV-0002)

Purpose: Maintains vessel operating pressure within 9.0-10.4 barg range by modulating gas flow to HP Flare.

Loop Components:

PIT-0002: Pressure transmitter at Nozzle N16 senses vessel pressure

PIC-0002: Controller compares measured pressure to setpoint

PV-0002: 8-inch control valve on line 8"-PP01-362-GF0003-B01E9-PP modulates gas flow

Control Action:

Increasing vessel pressure → PIC-0002 output increases → PV-0002 opens → More gas flows to flare → Pressure decreases

Decreasing vessel pressure → PIC-0002 output decreases → PV-0002 closes → Less gas flows to flare → Pressure increases

7.2 Level Control Loop (LIC-0001 → LV-0001)

Decreasing vessel pressure → PIC-0002 output decreases → PV-0002 closes → Less gas flows to flare → Pressure increases

7.2 Level Control Loop (LIC-0001 → LV-0001)

Purpose: Maintains vessel liquid level at Normal Liquid Level (800mm) by modulating liquid discharge.

Loop Components:

LIT-0001: Level transmitter at Nozzles N7A/N7B senses liquid level

LIC-0001: Controller compares measured level to NLL setpoint (800mm)

LV-0001: 1.5-inch control valve on N3 outlet bypass modulates liquid flow

Control Action:

Rising level → LIC-0001 output increases → LV-0001 opens → More liquid drains → Level decreases

Falling level → LIC-0001 output decreases → LV-0001 closes → Less liquid drains → Level increases

7.3 High Pressure Safety Instrumented Function (PZT → 2oo3 → ESD)

Purpose: Provides independent high-pressure protection to prevent vessel overpressure.

Loop Components:

PZT-0001A, PZT-0001B, PZT-0001C: Three independent pressure transmitters

2oo3 Voting Logic (362-004): Requires 2 of 3 transmitters to confirm high pressure

ESD System: Initiates protective actions

Safety Action: When 2 out of 3 transmitters detect pressure above the high-pressure trip setpoint, the voting logic sends a trip signal to the ESD system, which closes EZV-0001 and initiates other protective actions.

7.4 Level Safety Instrumented Function (LZT → 2oo3 → ESD)

Purpose: Provides independent level protection for both high-high and low-low level conditions.

7.4 Level Safety Instrumented Function (LZT → 2oo3 → ESD)

Purpose: Provides independent level protection for both high-high and low-low level conditions.

Loop Components:

LZT-0002A, LZT-0002B, LZT-0002C: Three independent GWR level transmitters

2oo3 Voting Logic (362-003 for HHLL, 362-007 for LLLL): Requires 2 of 3 transmitters to confirm level alarm

ESD System: Initiates protective actions

Safety Actions:

HHLL (1300mm): Voting logic 362-003 trips to prevent liquid carryover to downstream equipment

LLLL (300mm): Voting logic 362-007 trips to prevent pump cavitation or loss of seal

1.0 Main Equipment: Fuel Gas Knockout Drum (PP01-362-V-01)

This document provides a detailed technical breakdown of the Fuel Gas Knockout Drum, designated PP01-362-V-01, and its associated piping and instrumentation systems. The knockout drum serves as a critical process vessel, designed to remove entrained liquids from two primary fuel gas streams before the gas proceeds to further treatment. This initial section details the fundamental design parameters and material specifications of the vessel itself.

The core specifications for the PP01-362-V-01 vessel are summarized below.

This foundational data provides the context for the following analysis of the primary inlet piping systems that feed this drum.

2.0 Primary Inlet Piping Systems

The primary function of the PP01-362-V-01 knockout drum is to process two main fuel gas streams. These streams originate from the DS1 and DS3 scrapper receivers and are routed to the drum via two distinct 16-inch piping systems. This section deconstructs the flow path of each of these inlet lines.

3.1 Nozzle N1: PSV and Flare Header Connection

Nozzle N1 is the primary pressure relief outlet, a 14-inch line that splits to serve two parallel Pressure Safety Valves (PSVs) which discharge to the HP Flare Header.

• Main Line: 14 in PP 01 362 GF01 B 01 E9 PP, equipped with a 14" ball valve (HV04).

• Flow Path to PSV-01:

2. The 8" line connects to the inlet of PSV-01.

3. The outlet of PSV-01 connects to a 16" line (16in PP01 500 VK66 B01 B8) via ball valve HV39. A 1" drain valve (HB0038) is situated between the PSV and HV39.

3.2 Nozzle N2: Process Gas Outlet

Nozzle N2 is the main 16-inch process gas outlet, directing treated gas to the Fuel Gas Treatment Package, with parallel connections to the HP Flare Header for blowdown or pressure control.

• Main Line: 16 in PP01 362 GF02 B 01 E9 PP, originating from the nozzle and including a blind flange and a pressure gauge connection (PG 01, HV15).

5.1 Summary of Control Loops

The P&ID details for the PP01-362-V-01 drum indicate one primary, explicitly defined control loop and a comprehensive system for level monitoring and control.

• Pressure Control: The primary automated control loop manages the drum's operating pressure. As detailed in Section 3.10, pressure transmitter PIT02 (on Nozzle N16) sends a signal to PIC02, which in turn modulates control valve PV02 on the 8-inch process gas outlet line to the HP Flare Header.
